content:
Can I assume this is an IMAP account? What do you see when you view the Trash folder on your email provider's web mail interface? Are the errant messages visible there? If so, delete them there and try syncing Thunderbird again. If the messages reappear locally but not on the server, the local folder index may have become corrupt. This may fix it:

- Right-click on the folder and choose "Properties", then click the Repair button. Then compact the folder and hopefully things will show as expected. If not, let us know and there are some slightly more drastic possibilities.
creator: rednoise

content:
If your goal is to replicate her complete email setup, including all accounts, messages, folders, contacts, calendars, etc, your best bet is to copy her complete Profile from the old computer to the new. You can use a flash drive as a transfer vehicle. This article describes how:

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/moving-thunderbird-data-to-a-new-computer
creator: rednoise

content:
This is a copy&paste from an answer I gave to a similar question a while back (see https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/questions/1570858#answer-1805771 ) ...

=

I did a little internet searching, and I don't see any sign that GMX supports OAuth2, which would explain why it's not an available option for you. I did find these generic email settings that you can try. You may need to confirm this info with their tech support:

Incoming (IMAP) Server: imap.gmx.com Port: 993 Encrytion: SSL or encryption

Outgoing (SMTP) Server: mail.gmx.com Port: 587 Encryption: STARTTLS

Outgoing (SMTP) Server: mail.gmx.com Port: 465 Encryption: SSL/TLS

Please note: You can only use a TLS protocol if your email program uses TLS versions 1.2 or 1.3. For security reasons, we no longer support the older TLS versions 1.0 and 1.1.

Info: If a connection via port 993 is not possible, you can try port 143. Please note that this connection is not encrypted by default. You can achieve encryption via port 143 by activating STARTTLS in your email program (e.g. Thunderbird, Outlook or Apple Mail) and using a valid SSL/TLS certificate.

==

I got this from:

https://support.gmx.com/pop-imap/imap/server.html
creator: rednoise

content:
Vai in Menu > Impostazioni account e nella parte sinistra della finestra trascina il nome dell'account nella posizione che desideri

Funziona solo per l'ordine degli account, non per le singole cartelle.

content:
I haven't tried it yet, but seems to me that this would be effective:

Apply filter when: GETTING NEW MAIL

Match the following: Subject ISN'T: XOifdpjksdOnLKNiUfnjawe0ujncd09uu9u32n (or any nonsense string of your choice)

Perform these actions: COPY MESSAGE TO (the folder of your choice)

Worth a try?
creator: rednoise

content: P.S. - I tried it and it does work. Best to uncheck the "Run Manually" option box so you don't accidentally do that, which will copy everything in the Inbox to your chosen folder.
creator: rednoise

content: I will comment on question #1: To change the default, just click the desired account to be default. That's all you need to do. The delete button does as you stated; it deletes the account from thunerbird.
